app.title: Celestial Mechanics: an interactive n-body simulation

force.heading: Gravity
force.body: Every pair of bodies pulls on each other. The softening ε stops the force from blowing up when two bodies get too close.
force.footnote: Click any colored letter in the formula to jump to its slider. n = 2 is real gravity. n = 1 makes orbits fall apart. n > 2 makes orbits drift in flower shapes.

integrator.heading: Stepping forward in time
integrator.body: Each frame we move every body forward in small time steps. This method keeps the total energy stable over long runs.
integrator.footnote: Smaller Δt is more accurate but slower. Crank Δt up to watch the simulation break.

energy.heading: Total energy
energy.body: A closed system should keep the same total energy forever. The drift number below shows how much we are losing or gaining.

masses.heading: Body masses
masses.body: Each preset is tuned to start out stable. Nudge a mass and watch the orbits drift, fall in, or fly out.
